name: Plan and Route
description: Generate a plan from an objective and route it.

name: Agent Trace
description: Analyze agent execution incrementally to produce structured trace state, decision graph views, and risk control signals during a run.

name: Compare Options
description:
Compare alternatives, options, scenarios, or proposals in a structured and explicit way. Produces qualitative analysis (pros, cons, risks, assumptions) and quantitative scoring (multi-criteria) with a comparative summary and trade-offs.
This skill is the "compare without deciding" counterpart to decision.make. Use it when you need a transparent comparison artifact that a human or another skill will consume — without forcing a final recommendation.
Pipeline: (1) qualitative analysis per option, (2) multi-criteria scoring with comparative summary and trade-offs, (3) quality gate on the comparison.
Typical upstream: agent.option.generate, task.frame, or direct user input. Typical downstream: decision.make, human review, or analysis.risk-assess.

options.description: Alternatives to compare. Each option should have at minimum: id, label, description. May include key_attributes.

comparison_goal.description: What the comparison is for — frames which criteria matter and how to weight them. Examples: "Which cloud provider best fits our latency and cost constraints?", "Compare three vendor proposals for the CRM migration."

criteria.description: Explicit evaluation criteria. Each criterion: name, description, optional weight. If omitted, criteria are inferred from comparison_goal.

context.description: Background information, prior analysis, or domain knowledge relevant to the comparison.

constraints.description: Hard constraints that apply to the comparison (budget, time, regulation).

analyzed_options.description: Options enriched with qualitative analysis: pros, cons, risks, assumptions per option.

scored_options.description: Options with per-criterion scores, overall_score, strengths, weaknesses.

criteria_used.description: Final criteria applied, including any inferred ones. Each with name, description, and weight.

comparative_summary.description: Narrative comparison of the options explaining key differentiators.

tradeoffs.description: Explicit trade-offs between top candidates. Each entry names the tension and what is gained/lost.

comparison_quality.description: Quality assessment of the comparison: overall_score (0-1) and per-dimension scores.

criteria_coverage.description: Are the criteria comprehensive and relevant to the comparison goal?

analysis_depth.description: Are pros, cons, risks, and assumptions specific and grounded rather than generic?

score_consistency.description: Are the quantitative scores consistent with the qualitative analysis?

tradeoff_clarity.description: Are trade-offs explicit and actionable, not vague?

use_cases: Vendor or tool comparison before procurement decision
Architecture alternative evaluation
Strategy or scenario comparison
Any structured A-vs-B-vs-C analysis

name: Decompose Problem
description:
Divide a complex problem or topic into manageable analysis blocks to avoid monolithic reasoning and mixed dimensions. Selects a decomposition strategy, generates described components with dependencies, and validates the decomposition for gaps and overlaps.
Pipeline: (1) classify the best decomposition strategy, (2) split into components with dependencies and order, (3) quality gate on the decomposition.
Typical upstream: task.frame output or direct user input. Typical downstream: analysis.synthesize (per component), analysis.compare, decision.make, or any skill that benefits from scoped sub-problems.

problem.description: The problem or topic to decompose. Should be a clear statement.

context.description: Background information, prior analysis, or domain knowledge.

decomposition_mode.description: Preferred decomposition axis: themes | dimensions | phases | questions | stakeholders | risks. If omitted, step 1 selects the best strategy.

components.description: List of components with id, label, description, dependencies, and analysis_order.

gaps.description: Areas that may be missing from the decomposition.

decomposition_quality.description: Quality assessment: overall_score (0-1) and per-dimension scores.

mece_compliance.description: Are components mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive? Score low if significant gaps or overlaps exist.

component_clarity.description: Is each component well-described with clear scope boundaries?

dependency_coherence.description: Are dependencies between components logical and complete? No circular dependencies, no missing links.

granularity.description: Are components at the right granularity — not too coarse (monolithic) nor too fine (fragmented)?

use_cases: Break down a complex market analysis into dimensions
Decompose a technology migration into phases
Split a regulatory compliance review into themes
Structure a research agenda into key questions

name: Assess Risks
description:
Identify risks, fragile assumptions, and failure modes associated with a target artifact (decision, plan, analysis, proposal, draft). Introduces adversarial thinking to counter the implicit optimism of LLM-generated outputs.
Pipeline: (1) extract risks, assumptions, failure modes, and mitigations, (2) classify risks by category, (3) quality gate on the risk analysis.
Typical upstream: decision.make, analysis.synthesize, agent.plan.generate, or any skill producing an artifact that needs stress-testing. Typical downstream: decision.make (risk-informed), eval.validate, human review.

target.description: The artifact to analyze for risks. Can be a decision, plan, synthesis, proposal, or any structured object.

context.description: Background information or domain knowledge relevant to risk identification.

risk_scope.description: Focus area: broad | operational | strategic | regulatory | technical | financial. Defaults to broad.

identified_risks.description: Risks with id, description, category, severity_hint, related_assumptions.

risk_categories.description: Categories found, each with name and count of risks.

critical_assumptions.description: Fragile assumptions with id, statement, fragility_hint, related_risks.

failure_modes.description: Concrete failure scenarios with id, description, trigger_conditions, related_risks.

unknowns.description: Areas where risk assessment is limited due to missing information.

risk_quality.description: Quality assessment of the risk analysis: overall_score (0-1) and per-dimension scores.

risk_coverage.description: Are risks identified across relevant categories? No obvious blind spots?

assumption_depth.description: Are implicit assumptions surfaced, not just obvious ones?

failure_mode_specificity.description: Are failure modes concrete with trigger conditions, not vague "things could go wrong" statements?

mitigation_actionability.description: Are mitigation ideas specific and actionable rather than generic ("monitor the situation")?

use_cases: Stress-test a strategic decision before execution
Identify risks in a project plan
Surface hidden assumptions in a market analysis
Pre-mortem analysis on a product launch proposal

name: Synthesize
description:
Transform a heterogeneous collection of research materials into a structured, actionable synthesis artifact. Accepts items as pre-extracted text or as source references (url, pdf_path, fs_path, memory_key). Resolves sources, then produces the full synthesis in a single model call — summary, key points, insights, tensions, uncertainties, coverage, next steps, quality assessment, and a human-readable narrative.
Fast path (default): 2 steps, 1 LLM call. Comparable in latency to a direct chat interaction but with a stable output contract and full traceability.
Suitable as an intermediate block in longer agent workflows: feeds naturally into decision.compare_options, task.frame, or direct human review.

items.description: List of research items to synthesize. Each item must include `id` and either `content` (pre-extracted text) or `source_ref` (object with `type` and `location`). Optional per-item fields: title, type (article | report | note | web_page | search_result | agent_output | transcript | raw_text), source, relevance_hint, metadata.

topic.description: Topic or subject of the research. Anchors the synthesis. If omitted, the skill infers the dominant topic from the corpus.

goal.description: Intended use of the synthesis (e.g. "evaluate expansion into market X", "assess regulatory risk for hydrogen in Europe"). Influences relevance prioritization and next_steps suggestions.

focus.description: Optional extraction and synthesis focus hint. Examples: "prioritize risks", "prioritize contradictions", "executive summary", "emphasize opportunities".

max_detail.description: Output depth: brief | standard | detailed. Defaults to standard.

quality_profile.description: Quality gate stringency: standard | strict. Defaults to standard. standard: self-assessed quality in the synthesis call. strict: reserved for future grounding validation step.

summary.description: Executive summary of the corpus. Answers: what the material is about, what the key conclusions are, and what seems most important.

key_points.description: Consolidated list of stable findings or claims that appear central or recurrent across sources. Each entry is a concise statement.

insights.description: Interpretive or non-trivial observations not captured by key_points. Patterns, implications, and differential signals worth noting.

tensions.description: Points where two or more items conflict, diverge in emphasis, or carry incompatible signals on the same topic. Each entry names the tension and the diverging positions.

uncertainties.description: Areas that are unclear, weakly supported, or missing from the corpus. Each entry is a statement of what is not known or not well established.

source_coverage.description: Coverage metadata: total_items, processed_items, coverage_ratio, dominant_subset_warning (true if a small subset dominates the synthesis), item_type_distribution.

next_steps.description: Suggested continuation actions. Examples: investigate an unresolved area, contrast a tension, compare options, pass to decision.compare_options.

synthesis_quality.description: Self-assessed quality block: confidence_score (0-1), coverage_ratio, evidence_strength, tension_density, uncertainty_load, quality_notes, assessment_passed.

human_readable.description: Narrative prose version of the synthesis suitable for direct human consumption without further post-processing.

input.instruction:
You are a research synthesis engine. You receive a collection of source items (already resolved to text) plus optional anchors in the constraints object: topic, goal, focus. Produce the complete synthesis in one pass.
Heuristics: - Elevate a claim to key_point if it appears across multiple items or is
  clearly central to the topic.
- Elevate a singular but important differential signal to insight. - Mark a tension when two items push in opposite directions on the same
  theme. Include topic, position_a, position_b.
- Mark an uncertainty when a claim is weakly supported or a relevant area
  is absent from the corpus.
- Suggest next_steps aligned to goal if provided, otherwise suggest
  generally useful follow-ups.
- source_coverage: count total_items and processed_items from the input,
  compute coverage_ratio, flag dominant_subset_warning if a small subset
  dominates, list item_type_distribution.
- human_readable: a narrative prose version (3-8 paragraphs depending on
  detail level) suitable for direct human consumption.
- synthesis_quality: self-assess with confidence_score =
  0.35 * coverage_ratio + 0.35 * evidence_strength
  - 0.15 * tension_density - 0.15 * uncertainty_load, clamped [0, 1].
  Set assessment_passed = true if confidence_score >= 0.4 and
  coverage_ratio >= 0.5.

Rules: - Do not fabricate claims not traceable to the source items. - Do not repeat the full source text in the output. - Respect the detail level for depth of each field. - If topic is not provided, infer the dominant topic from the corpus.

use_cases: Consolidate multiple research documents into a structured synthesis
Integrate agent findings before passing to a decision workflow
Compress research context without losing key signals
Prepare a human-readable briefing from heterogeneous source material
Feed structured synthesis output into decision.compare_options

input.topic: Market expansion opportunity in sector X
input.goal: Evaluate whether to expand into market X

output.summary: The expansion appears viable given demand signals but faces regulatory uncertainty and high entry cost.
key_points: Entry cost significantly above initial estimates
insights: Execution risk, not market fit, is the primary constraint

tensions.position_a: Low competition observed in current data
tensions.position_b: Emerging entrants signal rising competitive intensity

uncertainties: No consolidated data on total cost of entry
next_steps: Validate regulatory timeline with legal team
Pass to decision.compare_options with entry vs defer scenarios

synthesis_quality.quality_notes: Synthesis is well-grounded but regulatory uncertainty reduces confidence.

name: Transcribe Summary
description: Transcribe audio and summarize the transcript.

name: Diff Summary
description: Extract a code diff and summarize it.

name: Parse and Validate
description: Parse JSON text and validate it against a schema.

name: Make Decision
description:
Take a problem, context, and available information and produce an explicit, structured, justified decision under uncertainty. Closes the gap between "analysis" and "actionable decision".
Pipeline: (1) generate options if not provided, (2) qualitative analysis of pros, cons, risks, and assumptions per option, (3) multi-criteria quantitative scoring, (4) select recommendation with structured justification, failure modes, and next steps, (5) self-assess decision quality.
Designed to be observable at each step. Each intermediate result is inspectable via agent.trace. Favors a slower, transparent pipeline over a single opaque LLM call. Zero uses of model.output.generate — every step is a dedicated, named capability.
Typical upstream: analysis.synthesize output as context_items. Typical downstream: task planning, human review, or execution.

goal.description: Clear description of the problem or decision to resolve. Examples: "Should we expand to market X?", "Which architecture should we adopt for the new platform?"

context_items.description: Relevant information: outputs from analysis.synthesize, notes, data, analyses. Each item should have at least a content field (string). If omitted, the skill works from the goal alone (lower confidence expected).

options.description: Explicit options to evaluate. Each option should have at minimum: id, label, description. If omitted, step 1 generates 2-4 plausible options from goal and context.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the decision must respect: budget, time, regulation, resources, organizational limits.

risk_tolerance.description: Risk appetite: low | medium | high. Defaults to medium. Affects how uncertainty and downside scenarios are weighted throughout the pipeline.

recommendation.description: The final recommendation. Must be clear, concrete, and unambiguous. Never "it depends" or "consider your options".

alternatives_considered.description: List of all options evaluated, including generated ones if applicable.

criteria_used.description: Criteria used for evaluation, with weights.

evaluation_summary.description: Comparative summary of how options scored against criteria.

tradeoffs.description: Explicit trade-offs of the chosen recommendation. What is gained, what is sacrificed.

confidence_score.description: Numeric confidence in the recommendation (0.0 to 1.0).

uncertainties.description: What is unclear, missing, or weakly supported. Honest enumeration of unknowns that could affect the decision.

failure_modes.description: Conditions under which the recommendation could fail. Examples: regulatory change, cost escalation, incorrect assumptions.

next_steps.description: Recommended follow-up actions: validations, pilots, additional analysis, execution steps.

decision_quality.description: Self-assessment of the decision process: input_quality (0-1), criteria_coverage (0-1), analysis_consistency (0-1), overall_score (0-1), quality_notes (string), assessment_passed (boolean).

human_readable.description: Narrative prose version of the decision suitable for direct human consumption without further post-processing.

input_quality.description: Was the decision based on sufficient, relevant input? Score low if context_items were absent or sparse.

criteria_coverage.description: Were the evaluation criteria comprehensive and relevant to the goal? Score low if obvious criteria were missed.

analysis_consistency.description: Is the recommendation consistent with the evaluation scores and trade-off analysis? Flag contradictions.

justification_depth.description: Are the uncertainties, failure modes, and next steps specific and actionable, not generic boilerplate?

use_cases: Strategic decisions (market entry, technology selection, initiative prioritization)
Operational decisions (implementation approach, vendor selection, architecture choice)
Decisions under uncertainty (incomplete scenarios, contradictory information, complex trade-offs)
Agent pipeline closure (final step after analysis.synthesize, replacing vague responses)

examples.goal: Should we expand to market X?

context_items.content: Market research synthesis indicating strong demand but high regulatory uncertainty...

expected_outputs.recommendation: Proceed with a controlled pilot entry in market X, limited to segment Y

name: Chunk and Embed
description: Split text into chunks and generate embeddings for the original text.

name: Read Email Summary
description: Read emails from a mailbox and summarize their content.

name: Validate Artifact
description:
Check whether an output or artifact meets a set of quality conditions: structural completeness, internal consistency, and coverage of key areas. Produces a clear pass/fail per check with actionable recommended fixes.
Generic validation skill — works on any structured artifact (synthesis, decision, plan, report, etc.). Replaces domain-specific quality-assess skills with a single composable pipeline.
Pipeline: (1) structural validation against schema, (2) semantic consistency and grounding checks, (3) coverage and quality scoring with rubric.
Typical upstream: any skill that produces an artifact to validate. Typical downstream: human review, retry with fixes, or release gate.

artifact.description: The artifact to validate. Any structured object — synthesis, decision, plan, comparison, report.

validation_goal.description: What the validation is checking for. Frames which checks matter. Examples: "Verify this synthesis is complete and grounded", "Check this decision for consistency and coverage".

checklist.description: Explicit list of checks to perform. Each check: name, description, severity (error | warning). If omitted, checks are inferred from validation_goal and artifact structure.

quality_rules.description: Custom rubric dimensions and weights for the coverage/quality scoring step. If omitted, sensible defaults are inferred.

context.description: Background information or evidence to check the artifact against (e.g., source materials for grounding validation).

checks_performed.description: All checks that were run, each with: name, description, result (pass | fail | warning), and detail.

failed_checks.description: Checks that failed, with detail on what was wrong.

coverage_gaps.description: Key areas or dimensions that are missing or underrepresented.

validation_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the validation result.

recommended_fixes.description: Actionable fix suggestions for each failed check or warning.

validation_passed.description: True if all error-severity checks passed. Warnings do not block.

use_cases: Validate a synthesis artifact before delivery
Check a decision for internal consistency
Verify a plan covers all required areas
Generic output quality gate before human review

name: Caption and Classify Image
description: Generate a caption for an image and classify it.

name: Retrieve Summary
description: Retrieve a memory value and summarize it.

name: Store Embedding
description: Generate an embedding from text and store it in memory.

name: Store Summary
description: Summarize text and store the summary in memory.

name: PDF Chunk and Embed
description: Read a PDF, chunk its text, and generate embeddings for the full text.

name: Read PDF Keyword Summary
description: Read a PDF, extract keywords, and summarize its text.

name: Read PDF Summary
description: Read a PDF and summarize its text.

name: Generate Research Briefing
description:
Build a structured intelligence briefing from web sources. Searches the web for a given query, normalizes results into corpus format, optionally fetches full page content (deep mode), extracts keywords and entities, summarizes the corpus, clusters information into thematic groups, and assesses risks.
Pipeline: 8 steps, 0 model.output.generate calls. All analysis is performed by dedicated capabilities with stable contracts. Steps 5a-5c run in parallel.
Modes:
  quick (default) — works with search result snippets, lower latency.
  deep — fetches full page content via research.source.retrieve, richer output.

query.description: Search query describing the market, topic, or domain to research. Examples: "green hydrogen market Europe", "pilates boutique fitness Spain".

mode.description: "quick" (default) uses search snippets. "deep" fetches full page content for richer analysis at the cost of higher latency.

num_sources.description: Maximum number of sources to process (default 8).

focus.description: Optional focus hint for clustering and risk analysis. Examples: "competition and pricing", "regulatory landscape", "technology trends".

theme_hints.description: Optional theme labels to guide clustering. If omitted, defaults to ["market_overview", "key_players", "trends", "risks", "opportunities"].

summary.description: Executive summary of the briefing corpus.

entities.description: Extracted named entities (companies, people, organizations, locations).

clusters.description: Thematic clusters. Each contains: theme, description, item_ids, summary, signal_strength. Default themes: market_overview, key_players, trends, risks, opportunities.

risks.description: Identified risks with severity hints and related assumptions.

assumptions.description: Fragile assumptions underlying the briefing content.

failure_modes.description: Concrete failure scenarios related to the briefing subject.

sources.description: Normalized source list with URLs, titles, and metadata.

quality.description: Quality assessment of the briefing output.

source_coverage.description: Are enough sources represented? Is the briefing based on diverse, non-redundant material?

thematic_completeness.description: Do clusters cover the expected dimensions (overview, players, trends, risks, opportunities)? Are any major themes missing?

signal_specificity.description: Are keywords, entities, and cluster summaries specific and actionable rather than generic?

risk_depth.description: Are risks grounded in the source material? Are assumptions explicit and failure modes concrete?

use_cases: Generate market intelligence briefing from web sources
Research competitive landscape for a given industry or market
Produce a structured overview of an emerging technology or trend
Assess risks and opportunities in a new market segment

examples.description: Quick briefing on green hydrogen market

output.summary: The European green hydrogen market is accelerating...

risks.description: Subsidy dependency may create cliff risk

examples.description: Deep briefing on boutique fitness competition

name: Normalize Corpus
description: Normalize a heterogeneous collection of research items into a clean, uniform representation ready for analysis. Handles text extraction from raw content, chunking of long items, deduplication of near-identical entries, and language detection. Items may arrive as pre-extracted text or as source references (url, pdf_path, fs_path, memory_key) which are resolved during normalization.

items.description: List of input items to normalize. Each item must include an `id` and either a `content` string (pre-extracted text) or a `source_ref` object with `type` and `location` fields for lazy extraction. Optional fields per item: title, type (article | report | note | web_page | search_result | agent_output | transcript | raw_text), source, relevance_hint, metadata.

max_chunk_size.description: Maximum character length per chunk when splitting long items. Items shorter than this threshold are not split. Defaults to a runtime-defined value.

normalized_items.description: List of normalized items, each containing: id, content (extracted text), type, language, chunks (array), source, title, and any retained metadata.

normalization_stats.description: Summary statistics of the normalization pass including total_input, extracted_count, chunked_count, deduplicated_count, and language_distribution.

input.instruction: Return one object with exactly two top-level fields: normalized_items and normalization_stats. normalized_items must be an array where each item includes: id, content, type (inferred from input if not provided, defaulting to raw_text), language, chunks, source, title, and any retained metadata. normalization_stats must include: total_input, extracted_count, chunked_count, deduplicated_count, and language_distribution. Do not rename these fields, omit them, or wrap them under any additional structure.

use_cases: Prepare heterogeneous research material for downstream analysis
Normalize agent outputs, PDFs, web pages, and notes into a uniform format
Deduplicate and chunk long documents before synthesis

name: Quality Assess
description: Assess the quality of a research synthesis artifact by measuring corpus coverage, evidence strength, tension density, and uncertainty load. Produces a synthesis_quality block with a composite confidence_score, per-dimension scores, and human-readable quality_notes. Also validates the synthesis structure and emits actionable quality_warnings when thresholds are not met. Intended as a reusable quality gate for any analytical output that aggregates multiple research items.

synthesis_object.description: The structured synthesis artifact to assess. Expected to contain at minimum: summary, key_points, uncertainties, source_coverage.

findings_by_item.description: Per-item findings as produced by research.extract-findings. Used to compute evidence_strength, tension_density, and uncertainty_load from the source data.

normalized_items.description: The normalized item list as produced by research.normalize-corpus. Used for coverage_ratio computation (items used vs items provided).

quality_profile.description: Quality gate stringency: standard | strict. Defaults to standard. strict raises thresholds for evidence_strength and coverage_ratio and adds additional grounding validation.

synthesis_quality.description: Quality assessment block containing: confidence_score (0-1), coverage_ratio (0-1), evidence_strength (0-1), tension_density (0-1), uncertainty_load (0-1), quality_notes (string), and dimensions (object with per-dimension pass/fail and observed values).

quality_warnings.description: List of actionable warnings when quality dimensions fall below thresholds. Each warning includes dimension, observed_value, threshold, and message.

assessment_passed.description: True if all required quality dimensions meet the threshold defined by quality_profile. False triggers a retry or escalation recommendation.

coherence.description: Does the summary and key_points read as a coherent integrated view?

coverage.description: Are the key themes from source items represented in the synthesis?

grounding.description: Are claims supported by the findings rather than fabricated?

caution.description: Are uncertainties and tensions surfaced rather than hidden?

input.instruction:
Using the provided context bundle, compute the final synthesis_quality block. The context includes synthesis_object, findings_by_item, normalized_items, quality_profile, eval_score, eval_dimensions, grounding_valid, grounding_issues, confidence_adjustment, and grounding_rationale. Compute: coverage_ratio (items with at least one finding used in synthesis divided by total normalized items), evidence_strength (average across all findings_by_item claim evidence_strength mapped to 0=weak, 0.5=moderate, 1=strong), tension_density (number of items with conflicting claims on the same theme divided by total items, capped at 1), uncertainty_load (items with uncertainty_signals divided by total items). confidence_score = 0.35 * coverage_ratio + 0.35 * evidence_strength
  - 0.15 * tension_density - 0.15 * uncertainty_load, clamped to [0, 1].
Apply confidence_adjustment from grounding validation if provided. Write quality_notes explaining the score in 1-3 sentences. Apply quality_profile thresholds: standard requires confidence_score >= 0.4 and coverage_ratio >= 0.5; strict requires confidence_score >= 0.6 and coverage_ratio >= 0.7. Emit quality_warnings for any dimension below threshold. Set assessment_passed accordingly. Return exactly three top-level fields: synthesis_quality, quality_warnings, and assessment_passed.

use_cases: Gate a research synthesis before it is used in a decision workflow
Detect low-coverage or poorly grounded synthesis outputs
Produce a reusable confidence score for any multi-source analytical artifact

name: Filter Table Summary
description: Filter a table and summarize the filtered rows.

name: Frame Task
description:
Take an ambiguous or broad task and convert it into a structured problem definition: what needs to be solved, what type of work it is, what information is needed, and what approach to follow.
Prevents the agent from starting work on a misinterpreted brief. Acts as the entry point to the system — every complex workflow benefits from framing first.
Pipeline: (1) classify the task type, (2) extract objectives, constraints, and information needs, (3) propose an approach, (4) suggest which skills should run next.
Typical upstream: direct user input or agent intake. Typical downstream: analysis.decompose, analysis.synthesize, analysis.compare, decision.make, or any domain-specific skill.

task.description: The task description as given. May be vague, broad, or well-defined. Examples: "We need to decide on a cloud provider", "Prepare a market analysis for Southeast Asia", "Review the Q3 incident reports and recommend process changes".

context.description: Background information, organizational context, or prior outputs that help interpret the task.

constraints.description: Known constraints: budget, time, regulation, team capacity, etc.

goal_hint.description: Optional hint about the desired outcome type or direction, when the task description alone is ambiguous.

problem_statement.description: Clear, operationalized restatement of the task. Unambiguous, scoped, and actionable.

task_type.description: Classification of the work: analysis | decision | comparison | drafting | validation | research | planning | other.

objectives.description: Explicit objectives extracted or inferred from the task. Each objective is a string stating what must be achieved.

constraints_extracted.description: Structured constraints: explicit ones from input plus inferred ones. Fields may include budget, time, regulation, resources, scope.

information_needed.description: What information appears necessary to solve the task well. Each item describes a gap and optionally suggests how to fill it.

recommended_approach.description: Proposed general approach to tackle the task. Not a detailed plan — a directional strategy (e.g., "decompose into market dimensions, then synthesize per dimension, then compare").

suggested_next_skills.description: Ordered list of skill IDs that should execute after framing. Each entry includes skill_id and a brief rationale.

problem_statement.description: Clear, operationalized restatement of the task.

information_needed.description: What information is missing and how to get it.

use_cases: Interpret an ambiguous user request before starting work
Structure a complex brief into actionable components
Route a task to the right combination of analytical skills
Entry point for any multi-skill workflow

name: Detect Language and Classify

name: Entity Summary

name: Keyword and Summary Extraction

name: Language Summary

name: Translate Summary

name: Fetch and Classify

name: Fetch and Summarize Web Page

name: Web Page Chunk and Embed

name: Page Summary

name: Search Summary
description: Search the web and summarize the search results.
